text:
The weighted average price is computed as the sum of (price × qty) divided by the sum of qty.  

Sum of (price × qty):  
10×3 = 30  
20×2 = 40  
15×5 = 75  
8×7 = 56  
25×1 = 25  
Total = 30 + 40 + 75 + 56 + 25 = 226  

Sum of qty:  
3 + 2 + 5 + 7 + 1 = 18  

Weighted average = 226 / 18 ≈ 12.555...  
Rounded down to nearest integer = 12  

The previous output matches.  

```python
data = [(10,3),(20,2),(15,5),(8,7),(25,1)]
total_price_qty = sum(p * q for p, q in data)
total_qty = sum(q for _, q in data)
result = int(total_price_qty / total_qty)
print(result)
```
